mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprrice growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) signals demand destruction and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.3pp within 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.5 index points signals expanding productive capacity and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO > 105.5 are not significantly higher than when INDPRO ≤ 105.5 (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,100 thousand units combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 244) signals demand weakness and monetary policy constraints, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of HOUST and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than when UNRATE ≤ 4.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), where real rates turn negative (FEDFUNDS < inflation growth rate), signals monetary accommodation that erodes savings incentives and redirects capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) above 475,000 million dollars combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS > 146,000 thousand) signals robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings growth expectations accelerate.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of RSAFS and PAYEMS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate logistic regression over any rolling 12-month window.
